summary: The session focused on Charles F. Reynolds III, M.D., a prominent geriatric psychiatrist known for his research in mood disorders among older adults. Key topics included his academic credentials, clinical background, awards received, and potential contributions to the DSM-5.
facts: Charles F. Reynolds III, M.D., earned his undergraduate degree from Yale University and his medical degree from the Johns Hopkins University School of Medicine. He completed his residency in psychiatry at the University of Pittsburgh's Western Psychiatric Institute and Clinic. Dr. Reynolds is a board-certified psychiatrist specializing in geriatric psychiatry. He has received the American College of Psychiatrists' Award for Research in Geriatric Psychiatry for his significant achievements in geriatric psychiatry research. He has also been awarded the American Association for Geriatric Psychiatry's Distinguished Scientist Award for his lasting contributions to the field. Dr. Reynolds has focused his clinical work on the diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of mood and sleep disorders in older adults. He has been involved in various clinical settings, providing direct patient care and working with interdisciplinary teams. As a professor at the University of Pittsburgh School of Medicine, he has contributed to training and mentoring future geriatric psychiatrists. There is no specific information available about Dr. Reynolds' direct contribution to the DSM-5.
